objectives: Recognize the importance of the motherland

Reflect on values like truth, non-violence, and compassion
Describe the author's first encounter with Nehru

Identify the characteristics of the rain wind

Appreciate the lively imagery of the wind
Explain how the wind brings joy and freshness

Understand why choosing good friends is crucial
Recognize the influence of company on character

Understand the negative effects of bad company
Learn to distinguish between good and bad influences
Understand Kabir's philosophy on selflessness and humility
Reflect on the transient nature of life

Identify Tulsidas's views on good company

Understand Rahim's advice on preserving relationships
Recognize the value of small things and people

Identify the items they used for protection
Understand why the eggs were broken

Describe the scholar's confusion about the oil mill's ox

Compare the scholar's complex thinking with the owner's practicality

Describe Mohan's symptoms and his parents' concern

chapters.title_en: I Wish To Be The Smallest

Understand the perceived loss of intimacy and care with age

Trace his academic path and achievements

Appreciate his lasting impact as a freedom fighter and social worker

Identify its rejection of adornment and worldly glory
Explain the flower's ultimate wish
Describe its desire to be sacrificed for the motherland

Compare the treatment of Indian freedom fighters with British officers
Recognize his appeal for communal harmony

Reflect on the ultimate price paid for freedom
